empty-response.description: Response returned when there is no content.

text.description:
The text content of the message to be sent.
Maximum length: 10,000 characters.
text.example: New interns will be joining these teams from July.

card.description: An optional object that defines the visual presentation of the message card.

title.description: The title of the message card.

theme.description:
The visual theme of the message card.
Allowed values: modern-inline | poll | prompt

thumbnail.description: The URL of the thumbnail image for the message card.

slides.description: An array of structured content elements to be included in the message.

type.description:
The type of content.
Allowed values: table

data.description: The actual content data corresponding to the specified type.

headers.description: An array of table header names for the table columns.

rows.description:
Represents the data rows to be displayed in the table. Each row must be defined as a JSON object containing key-value pairs, where:
  • The key must exactly match one of the values defined in the headers array.
  • The value represents the corresponding cell data for that column.
  • Each object in the array represents one table row.
Syntax example for rows:
For every row object,
{
"<Header1>": "<RowValue1>",
"<Header2>": "<RowValue2>",
...
}
  • Ensure that the keys in each row object match the header names defined in the headers array.
  • The number of keys in each row object should align with the number of headers to maintain table integrity.

styles.description: Defines the visual styling and layout behavior of the table.

width.description:
• Specifies the width distribution of each column in percentage.
  • The number of values must match the number of columns defined in headers.
  • The sum of all values must equal 100.

sticky.description:
Freezes specific rows and columns so they remain visible while scrolling.
Note: A maximum of 2 rows and 2 columns can be frozen.

rows.description: Number of rows to be frozen.

columns.description: Number of columns to be frozen.

text.description:
The text content of the message to be sent.
Maximum length: 10,000 characters.
text.example: Welcome to Agile Bot! I'm here to give you a brief on what Agile is all about.

card.description: An optional object that defines the visual presentation of the message card.

title.description: The title of the message card.

theme.description:
The visual theme of the message card.
Allowed values: modern-inline | poll | prompt

thumbnail.description: The URL of the thumbnail image for the message card.

slides.description: An array of structured content elements to be included in the message.

type.description:
The type of content.
Allowed values: list

data.description:
Represents the list items to be displayed in the message.
Each item in the array must be provided as a string, where each string represents a single list entry.
example: Identify and work on a fix for bugs instantly

styles.description: Defines the visual styling of the list.

type.description:
Specifies the bullet or numbering style applied to the list.
Allowed values: disc | circle | square | decimal | lower-alpha | upper-alpha | lower-roman | upper-roman

text.description:
The text content of the message to be sent.
Maximum length: 10,000 characters.
text.example: Hi Team! Take a look at the monthly financial reports attached.

card.description: An optional object that defines the visual presentation of the message card.

title.description: The title of the message card.

theme.description:
The visual theme of the message card.
Allowed values: modern-inline | poll | prompt

thumbnail.description: The URL of the thumbnail image for the message card.

slides.description: An array of structured content elements to be included in the message.

type.description:
The type of content.
Allowed values: label

data.description:
Represents the key-value details displayed inside the label.
Each item in the array must be a JSON object containing a key-value pair, where:
  • The key represents the field name.
  • The value represents the corresponding field value.
Syntax for key-value pairs in data array: "data":[{"Key1": "Value1"},{"Key2": "Value2"},...]

text.description:
The text content of the message to be sent.
Maximum length: 10,000 characters.
text.example: Please find the attached image for the office layout.

card.description: An optional object that defines the visual presentation of the message card.

title.description: The title of the message card.

theme.description:
The visual theme of the message card.
Allowed values: modern-inline | poll | prompt

thumbnail.description: The URL of the thumbnail image for the message card.

slides.description: An array of structured content elements to be included in the message.

type.description:
The type of content.
Allowed values: image

data.description:
Represents the list of image URLs to be displayed in the message card.
Each item in the array must be a publicly accessible image URL. The URLs should point directly to image resources (e.g., .png, .jpg, .jpeg, .webp).

text.description:
The text content of the message to be sent.
Maximum length: 10,000 characters.
text.example: Hi Team! Please find the sales performance chart for this quarter.

card.description: An optional object that defines the visual presentation of the message card.

title.description: The title of the message card.

theme.description:
The visual theme of the message card.
Allowed values: modern-inline | poll | prompt

thumbnail.description: The URL of the thumbnail image for the message card.

slides.description: An array of structured content elements to be included in the message.

type.description:
The type of content.
Allowed values: percentage_chart

data.description:
Represents the dataset to be displayed in the chart.
Each item in the array corresponds to one segment in the chart.
Maximum Items: 5

label.description:
Defines the category name for the chart segment (what the segment represents).
Maximum length: 20 characters.

value.description:
Represents the numerical value of the chart segment.
The size of each segment is calculated relative to other values in the dataset.

styles.description:
Defines the visual style or appearance of the chart.
The style value must be provided using the preview attribute

preview.description: Specifies the chart visualization style. Allowed values: pie | doughnut | semi_doughnut

text.description:
The text content of the message to be sent.
Maximum length: 10,000 characters.
text.example: Exploring how our customers have been leveraging integrations in the past week!

card.description: Optional object that defines the visual presentation of the message card.

title.description: The title of the message card.

theme.description: The visual theme of the message card.

slides.description: An array of structured content elements to be included in the message.

type.description:
Specifies the type of visualization to be generated.
Allowed values: graph

styles.description: Specifies the visual styling configuration of the graph.

preview.description:
Specifies the rendering style of the graph.
Allowed values: vertical_bar | vertical_stacked_bar | trend

x_axis.description:
Represents the label for the x-axis of the graph.
Maximum length: 20 characters.

y_axis.description:
Represents the label for the y-axis of the graph.
Maximum length: 20 characters.

data.description:
Represents the dataset to be plotted on the graph. Each item represents one category.
Maximum items: 5

category.description:
Specifies the category name for which data is represented (for example: Products, Months, Integrations).
Maximum length: 20 characters.

values.description:
Represents the list of data points within the category.
Maximum items: 20

label.description:
Specifies the identifier or name of the data point.
Maximum length: 20 characters.

value.description: Specifies the numeric value associated with the label. This value is used to plot the graph.

example.text: Exploring how our customers have been leveraging integrations in the past week!
